_doc: Acceptance-gate eval corpus for text-search engines, scoped to ley-line-open at HEAD. Each entry maps one natural-language `query` to a non-empty set of `expected_node_ids` (paths relative to repo root). Consumed by `tests/acceptance_gates.rs`, which computes NDCG@10 (binary relevance) against the WitchcraftEngine when `engine-witchcraft` and `WITCHCRAFT_ASSETS_DIR` are both set, and asserts `mean >= NDCG_BASELINE`. The constant default (0.40) is a placeholder — see the doc-comment on `NDCG_BASELINE` for the bump protocol. Expansion to ≥30 queries is a TODO required before tightening the threshold; the 10 below are the seed set, hand-picked to span ADRs, code modules, and README content.

queries.query: how are cap'n proto schemas versioned and what is the file id allowlist
what is the merkle CAS substrate and how does BLAKE3 root chaining work
sidecar vector index that does not survive sqlite3 serialize
embedding pass enrichment that fills the vector sidecar from nodes
daemon protocol fixture drift gate JSON
tree-sitter AST projection and bidirectional splice
hyperdimensional computing sheaf stalks heyting algebra
chat embed semantic search over claude code sessions
distroless OCI image build with krust and chainguard static
